Box.Box: Misfits: Nuclear Wasteland Server Rules

Misfits Fallout is a roleplay server. The main goal of this server is not only to provide engaging gameplay, but also to create stories that players can share even with friends outside of standard Space Station 14.
Note: your own skills and actions will determine the path of your story. The Wasteland is a harsh place. Do not expect help from the gods except in the most extreme cases.
Rule 0: Don't be a jerk.

Not everyone likes this rule, but it is necessary. This rule covers players who show excessive toxicity and play exclusively for personal gain. Those who play only for themselves and try to "win" at any cost, suppressing others and ignoring the rules.
Do not be a toxic player. Our server is a space for collaborative creativity, not an arena for personal victories at any cost. Avoid actions that suppress other players or damage the game's atmosphere.
Respect the work of admins and developers. Remember that they are volunteers with their own lives outside the project. Be patient and constructive when communicating.
Cooperate when resolving issues. Do not disconnect during an admin ticket without a valid reason. If you encounter technical problems, report them within 15 minutes.
Administration reserves the right to make decisions in situations not covered by the rules if a player's behavior causes a negative reaction in the community.
Remember that the goal of the server is to create an engaging gameplay experience for all participants. Act in the spirit of mutual respect and common sense.
Violating these principles may lead to restricted access to the server. All punishments are determined at the administration's discretion with the situation's context in mind.
Rule 1: Griefing

Griefing, or intentionally ruining the gameplay experience for others, is strictly prohibited. This includes actions done solely to harm or inconvenience other players without any gain or reason other than to spoil the game.
A defining feature of griefing is the lack of personal benefit for the offender. This rule also covers events that cause significant damage to gameplay even if there was no intent to ruin the game for others. The simplest example is planting mines in players' homes.
Any actions that harm gameplay and spoil other players' experience, not covered by other rules, will be punished under this rule.
Rule 2: Fair play

2.1. Metagaming and use of meta information
Metagaming, meaning using or spreading in-game information through out-of-character communication or OOC chats, is strictly prohibited and punishable by a ban. This includes:
Getting information from OOC chats, observing as a ghost, messages in Discord, or streams.
Cooperative play (COOP) is metagaming and is punished especially harshly.
Streaming is not prohibited, but it is recommended to warn the administration about the intention to stream. Training co-op is allowed with prior notice to the administration.
Information obtained from any external sources or chats outside the game cannot be used by your character without violating this rule.

2.2. Multiaccounting
Using multiple SS14 accounts to gain an advantage is prohibited. This includes:
Using more than one account in the same round (multiboxing).
Ban evasion by creating a new account. Any attempt to enter the game with another account while the main one is banned is considered ban evasion.
Creating a new account to hide previous violations. Notify the administration if you created a new account or changed your current login.
Violating this rule is punishable by a permanent ban.

2.3. Unfair play
Using software or game flaws to gain an advantage in gameplay is prohibited. This includes:
Using bugs, cheats, scripts, clickers, and other third-party software is prohibited.
Intentionally creating significant load on the server by any means is prohibited.
If you find a bug, report it immediately in the appropriate Discord server channels.
Rule 3: Play roles and characters appropriate to the setting
Your character must fully fit the role and the Fallout universe setting.

3.1 Character behavior
Your character must behave appropriately for the setting. The game takes place in a post-apocalyptic future, and character behavior must reflect that. Your goal is to create a realistic and believable character that fits the Fallout theme.
When choosing a character name, stick to the Fallout theme. Avoid obvious references to real events or blatantly silly naming schemes.
Take your character creation seriously. Direct names of real-life figures, famous real or fictional characters, and silly names or names inappropriate for the character's species are forbidden. Administration may ask you to change a name if it does not follow the rules.
Copying character names for a character you or someone else played before their death or permanent imprisonment is forbidden without permission from the character's owner and/or the administration.

3.2 Interaction with chat channels
Do not abuse chat channels. This includes, but is not limited to: emotes, OOC, LOOC, Ahelp, or prayers. Abuse will lead to severe measures.
Do not break the fourth wall, do not use real-life memes or internet slang, and do not try to impose rules or resolve OOC problems in IC chat (use ahelp).
Using IC chats to discuss OOC topics (politics, memes, etc.) is prohibited. Emote chat is not intended for direct communication or for depicting political, erotic, or offensive actions.
Insults toward other players in OOC chats are prohibited, both direct and indirect. Using OOC chats to discuss political, extremist, erotic, and other sensitive topics is also prohibited.

3.3 Disrupting the game atmosphere
Do not draw attention to game mechanics, conventions, or admin actions that ruin the atmosphere or immersion.
Examples
If someone accidentally typed in IC chat with the wrong keyboard layout, do not draw attention to it. Just write the correct message.
If an admin accidentally hugged you or performed an action in the IC world, do not discuss it as admin actions.
Avoid shouting about "gods" or "admins" if something strange happens. Play it out as an anomaly or a strange event.
In game, treat admins as normal players without special privileges or excessive flattery.
3.3.1 LRP behavior
Avoid expressions and actions that do not fit the Fallout setting. Examples: "going SSD". If you need to leave the game, use an RP reason, such as "tired, going to sleep".
Do not climb over counters without necessity. If there is no bartender, call one or use a vending machine.
There are no rollbacks. If you accidentally typed something wrong, come up with an RP reason or apologize and write what you meant without emphasizing the mistake.
If someone messed up chat or layout, do not draw attention to it. It is an awkward situation best left alone.
Paper and other in-game media must not be used to create drawings with political, erotic, or offensive content.
3.3.2 Communication culture and atmosphere
Communicate in any language without breaking the atmosphere of the game. Avoid flooding, spam, and smileys in IC chat.
Maintain proper grammar and clarity of speech. Characters should speak according to their level of education. Avoid abbreviations like "pls" or "thx" and awkward LRP abbreviations if not justified by the role.
Write fully and stylistically appropriately. Try to create messages that are pleasant to read, following language norms.
Avoid excessive and unjustified insults. Expressions directed at the character are allowed (for example, "disgusting zombie"), but not at the player or their mental abilities. Repeated baseless insults may be considered meta-hate.
Remember that your speech affects the atmosphere of the game. Try to maintain immersion and respect other participants' experience.

3.4 Character appearance and description
Character appearance must fit the lore; exceptions are only possible with administration approval.
Character description should describe appearance; describing ongoing actions is allowed ("breathes heavily", "with a limp").
The description should be objective, without imposing subjective judgments ("beautiful eyes" -> "blue eyes").
The description should not list clothing; describing accessories is allowed.
Avoid excessive detail and heavy literary devices. This is examining a character, not studying them with a magnifying glass.

3.5 Game mechanics and RP
Do not specify a particular technology that needs research. That technology has not been researched within the round.
Try not to use game-mechanical terms such as "research points".
Your character is not all-knowing: their knowledge and skills have limits. Play the role you joined for, and do not teach others how to play their roles unless asked.

3.6 Slotting
Slotting - persistent ignoring of the surrounding world - is unacceptable. The world exists even without players: there will always be someone maintaining settlements and keeping order.
Violating this rule will be treated as LRP.
Examples
Discussing planned raids over the general radio channel.
Openly carrying contraband on NCR territory even if there are no players who can stop you.
Rule 4: Erotic roleplay (ERP) is not allowed

Erotic roleplay (ERP) is strictly prohibited. This includes any erotic stories written by your character in books, on paper, or other in-game media.
Use common sense: if an admin tells you to stop something that is "on the edge", you must stop immediately. Even if you are the only participant and nobody can see you, it is still a violation.
Punishment for violating this rule may not be immediate, but the fact of the violation will be discovered later. In most cases, violating this rule leads to a permanent ban.
However, sexual themes are allowed to a limited extent, including light sexual comments and light intimacy, such as kissing and hugging.
Examples of acceptable comments

"You look especially attractive today."
"You're a tempting devil."
Be careful and respect the boundaries of what is allowed.
Rule 5: SSD characters are protected from harmful hostile actions

Player characters who have disconnected and are considered "SSD" (Space Sleep Disorder) cannot be harmed, including direct physical attacks, kidnapping, or any other actions that directly harm the character. However, SSD characters can be robbed or have items stolen, as long as no harm is done to the SSD character.
Abusing this rule and disconnecting mid-fight is a punishable offense; characters who go SSD in the middle of a fight or escalation are not exempt from harm.
Faction members are not considered protected when they are SSD inside their faction base during a raid.
Rule 6: Armed conflicts and escalation

6.1 Armed conflicts
Talk first - then shoot! In almost any situation you should try to start dialogue first, and only then begin a firefight.
Exceptions: hunting fauna, chasing from an active RP situation, faction wars, direct threat to a character's life, event zones, and high-danger zones (for example, dungeons and open PVP zones).
During a robbery, do not take the victim's entire inventory. Leave at least one weapon (for example, a knife) and some food/medical items.
All Wasteland residents except raiders must have strong reasons to initiate a robbery.
If the victim did not offer armed resistance during a robbery, you are obliged to leave them alive.
If the victim tried to fight back, their life and property remain in the raider's hands.
Raiding faction bases by Wasteland residents is unacceptable under any circumstances. Faction members may only loot captured enemy bases during war.
Enslavement is an alternative to permanent imprisonment. For this reason it is allowed only as a punitive or voluntary measure, if it is allowed within the faction in principle.

6.2 Escalation rules
Escalation is required for all participants in a conflict.
Escalation must be established face to face; escalation over radio is invalid.
Escalation must last at least four seconds or until a response is received from the other side before attacking the enemy.
Good/valid escalation includes a demand that can be met or accepted by the person being escalated against, for example: "Give me your bag!" or "Leave here and you will live!".
Fleeing from escalation counts as a response and makes any attack against you by the escalating side valid.
Do not use escalation as a tool for free validhunting and justification for killing others at your discretion.
Escalate and engage in conflict within reasonable limits and moderately.

6.3 Behavior under crossfire
If you are caught in crossfire, you are NOT allowed to return fire unless you first escalate against the attackers.
As a participant in a conflict, minimize casualties and injuries among bystanders whenever possible.
Act realistically; avoid combat zones without a strong reason.
As a participant in a conflict, minimize casualties and injuries among bystanders whenever possible.

6.4 Early raids on faction bases
Do not raid an enemy faction base earlier than 30 minutes after the start of the round.
You may still attack members of that faction outside their base, but you are forbidden to enter their territory and start an attack until 30 minutes have passed.
This protection applies to all groups: Town, NCR, Brotherhood of Steel, Raiders, Rangers, Legion, etc.

6.5.1 Faction wars
All factions on the map are neutral toward each other at the start of the round.
The highest-ranking role currently on the map is considered the faction head and representative in the region. Only representatives from each faction may start escalations or wars/negotiations.
The reason for war (casus belli) must be substantial and clear; it can be a response to covert actions/attacks, capture of a point of interest, taking hostages, or conflicts of interest in expanding across the Wasteland.
A war is considered started only five minutes after faction representatives face-to-face announce their intention to begin hostilities. This provides a final chance for negotiations with an existing ultimatum before the war begins.

6.5.2 Faction wars
After capturing an enemy faction base, holding it, and with obvious lack of organized resistance, or by agreement of two faction representatives on each side, the war is considered ended.
Hostile actions against individual faction members in the Wasteland for reasons unrelated to faction membership are not considered the start of a war, but they may justify an ultimatum from one faction to another. Attempting to "trick" and destroy or significantly weaken a faction before the war begins is a violation of this rule.
Killing diplomats before, during, or immediately after negotiations is not allowed.
Killing players who have clearly surrendered and do not want to resist, in a situation without crossfire, is forbidden. This also applies to faction characters who arrive after the conflict begins and know nothing about its start.
Exceptions to escalation rules
Rescuing a faction mate wearing the appropriate uniform from the enemy.
Only faction members, or characters recruited with armbands, may participate in war. Only the highest-ranking member may recruit.
An enemy faction is besieging your headquarters/camp. This does NOT mean the enemy faction is at your gates. There must be an active conflict for you to intervene with weapons ready.
Rule 7: New Life Rules (NLR)

7.1. Within a round
Rejoining on the same character after their death or permanent imprisonment to bypass death or punishment is forbidden.
After revival, a character forgets the cause of death, circumstances, and the last 5 minutes before death. Ghosts do not retain memories of a past life. Switching from a ghost role to a normal role also does not retain any data or memories of the past life.
Attempts to bypass the rule through notes, indirect memories, or other tricks are punishable.

7.2. Between rounds
Characters may remember information about other characters only if it does not give clear advantages.
For example, an officer cannot grant access to a random vagrant because of IRL relationships.
Characters do not remember events that break the logic of the current round: death in another round, membership in another faction, etc.
When mentioning past events, avoid using other players' names, since in this round they may play other roles or may not want to be mentioned.
Additional points

The project lead, assistant lead, and senior moderator have the right to make decisions for any situation not mentioned in the rules.
All punishments for rule violations are set at their discretion.
The Misfits: Nuclear Wasteland server has a 16+ age limit. Nobody will check passports, of course, but violating this point may be considered grounds for a permanent ban.
At the moment, the rules are incomplete and significant changes are possible in the future.
